title: redesign: Landing Page Mid-Section — Trust Bar, Product Preview, Why AG, Rank Journey

labels.name: contributor-friendly
labels.description: Well-documented issue suitable for new contributors

body:
## What

Redesign the landing page sections between the Hero and CTA. The hero (top) and footer (bottom) are strong — the middle needs to match their quality.

## Current State

The current mid-section order is:
1. StatsSection (animated counters) — generic, doesn't tell a story
2. QuestShowcase (live quest cards) — functional but plain
3. BentoGrid (feature cards) — tries too much, no visual punch
4. HowItWorks (3-step text) — text-heavy, no illustrations

## New Section Order

Replace/upgrade to this flow:

### 1. Trust Bar (NEW — right after hero)
**File**: \`components/landing/TrustBar.tsx\` (create new)

A subtle horizontal strip that builds immediate credibility:
\`\`\`
Open Source (MIT)  ·  Backed by Open Paws  ·  [X] Adventurers  ·  [Y] Active Quests
\`\`\`
- Fetch real counts from \`/api/public/stats\`
- Single line, centered, \`bg-slate-950\` with \`border-b border-slate-800\`
- Small text (\`text-xs\`), subtle — not flashy
- Green dot before "Open Source" for alive feeling

### 2. Product Preview (NEW — replace StatsSection)
**File**: \`components/landing/ProductPreview.tsx\` (create new)

Show the product before explaining it. Two-column layout:
- **Left**: Large screenshot/mockup of the quest board (or use a styled div that looks like the quest board with 2-3 quest cards)
- **Right**: 3-4 bullet points:
  - "Browse real coding quests from companies"
  - "Claim work that matches your rank"
  - "Submit code, get reviewed, earn XP"
  - "Your Guild Card proves what you can ship"
- CTA button: "Browse the Quest Board"
- Dark background (\`bg-slate-950\`)

### 3. How It Works (REDESIGN existing)
**File**: \`components/landing/HowItWorks.tsx\` (modify)

Keep the 3-step structure but upgrade visually:
- Each step gets a styled icon container (not just a plain lucide icon)
- Add a connecting line/arrow between steps (orange gradient)
- Add subtle animation on scroll (already uses framer-motion)
- White background for visual rhythm break (already has this)
- Consider: numbered circles (01, 02, 03) with orange accent

### 4. Quest Showcase (KEEP — minor polish)
**File**: \`components/landing/QuestShowcase.tsx\` (modify)

Keep the live quest cards but:
- Add section heading: "Live Quests" with subtext "Real work from real companies"
- Improve card design: add difficulty badge color coding
- Add "View All Quests" CTA button at bottom
- If no quests available, show a compelling empty state

### 5. Why AG (NEW — competitive differentiation)
**File**: \`components/landing/WhyAG.tsx\` (create new)

4-column grid showing AG's unique value:

| Column | Title | Description |
|--------|-------|-------------|
| 1 | Verified Credentials | "Your Guild Card shows rank, quests, and quality scores — all verifiable by employers" |
| 2 | Real Paid Work | "Not toy problems. Real client tasks that ship to production" |
| 3 | RPG Progression | "F-Rank to S-Rank. Every quest completion levels you up" |
| 4 | AI-Augmented | "Use any tools. We measure delivery capability, not typing speed" |

- Each column: icon + title + 2-sentence description
- Dark background (\`bg-slate-900\`)
- Orange-500 icon accent
- Optional: subtle comparison row at bottom vs Upwork/Fiverr/LeetCode

### 6. Rank Journey (NEW — replace BentoGrid)
**File**: \`components/landing/RankJourney.tsx\` (create new)

Interactive F-to-S timeline showing what unlocks at each rank:

\`\`\`
F ---- E ---- D ---- C ---- B ---- A ---- S
¦      ¦      ¦      ¦      ¦      ¦      ¦
Tutorial  Bug   Features  Lead   Arch  Enterprise  Legend
Quests   Fixes           Teams  Work   Clients
\`\`\`

- Horizontal timeline with rank badges (use \`<RankBadge>\` component)
- Each rank node expands on hover/click to show: unlock description, XP needed, example quests
- Orange gradient line connecting ranks
- White or light background
- Mobile: vertical timeline

## Section Order in \`app/home/page.tsx\`

\`\`\`tsx
<Hero />
<TrustBar />
<ProductPreview />
<HowItWorks />     {/* redesigned */}
<QuestShowcase />  {/* polished */}
<WhyAG />
<RankJourney />
<CTASection />
<LogoMarquee />
\`\`\`

## Design Rules
- Orange-500 primary accent (one color only)
- Dark sections: \`bg-slate-950\` or \`bg-slate-900\`
- Light sections: white (\`bg-white\`) for visual rhythm
- Alternate dark/light for section contrast
- All data from \`/api/public/stats\` and \`/api/public/quests\` — no hardcoded fake data
- Mobile-first responsive
- Framer-motion scroll animations (already used in existing sections)

## Files to Create
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| \`components/landing/TrustBar.tsx\` | Create |
| \`components/landing/ProductPreview.tsx\` | Create |
| \`components/landing/WhyAG.tsx\` | Create |
| \`components/landing/RankJourney.tsx\` | Create |
| \`components/landing/HowItWorks.tsx\` | Modify |
| \`components/landing/QuestShowcase.tsx\` | Modify |
| \`components/landing/BentoGrid.tsx\` | Remove (replaced by WhyAG + RankJourney) |
| \`app/home/page.tsx\` | Modify — new section order |

## Context
- Current landing page: \`app/home/page.tsx\`
- Design system: \`components/ui/rank-badge.tsx\`, \`components/ui/xp-bar.tsx\`
- Public APIs: \`/api/public/stats\`, \`/api/public/quests\`
- See Discussion #141 for project roadmap
- Setup: \`docs/contributor-onboarding.md\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All new sections render correctly
- [ ] Data comes from public APIs (not hardcoded)
- [ ] Mobile responsive (test at 375px width)
- [ ] Smooth scroll animations
- [ ] Section contrast rhythm (dark/light alternating)
- [ ] \`npx tsc --noEmit\` passes clean
- [ ] BentoGrid removed, replaced by WhyAG + RankJourney

title: arch: Open Paws Platform Sync — Schema Alignment Between AG and OP
body:
## Context

The Open Paws Platform (https://github.com/Open-Paws/open-paws-platform) has built a **completely separate** guild implementation using Astro + Supabase. Their tables:
- \`guild_quests\` (separate from our \`quests\`)
- \`guild_quest_assignments\` (separate from our \`quest_assignments\`)
- Their own service layer in \`src/lib/services/guild/\`

They use status \`'applied'\` where we use \`'assigned'\`. They use \`quest_id\` (snake_case) where we use \`questId\` (camelCase in API, snake_case in DB via Prisma \`@map\`).

## Problem

The two systems have diverged:
- Our Phase 2 added: \`QuestTrack\`, \`QuestSource\`, \`pending_admin_review\`, \`needs_rework\`, Party/PartyMember models
- None of this exists in their Supabase schema
- Quest data is duplicated, not synced
- Changes on one platform don't reflect on the other

## Options to Evaluate

1. **API Gateway**: OP Platform calls our API instead of maintaining its own DB tables. Clean but requires auth bridging.
2. **DB Sync**: Scheduled sync between Neon and Supabase guild tables. Complex, fragile.
3. **Shared Schema Definition**: Both platforms read from the same schema definition, apply to their own DBs. Minimal coupling.
4. **Accept divergence**: OP Platform is a read-only view of AG quest data, synced manually. Simplest for now.

## Decision Needed
This needs a conversation between Abid and the OP Platform team (Sam?) about which approach to take before the May launch.

## Owner
@LarytheLord — architecture decision, not contributor work

title: arch: Bootcamp Pipeline End-to-End Test — Dry Run with 5 Students
body:
## What

Run the complete bootcamp pipeline manually with 5 test accounts to validate every code path before the real cohort arrives in May.

## Why

We have zero evidence the end-to-end flow works. Each component (onboard webhook, tutorial gating, quest claiming, submission, QA review, XP grant, rank-up) has been built and unit-tested, but the full lifecycle hasn't been exercised as an integrated pipeline.

## Test Script

### Step 1: Onboard 5 test students
Call the onboard webhook for 5 fake bootcamp students:
```bash
curl -X POST /api/onboard \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $BOOTCAMP_WEBHOOK_SECRET" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name":"Test Student 1","email":"test1@bootcamp.dev","bootcampStudentId":"BC001","cohort":"test-cohort-1","bootcampTrack":"beginner"}'
```
Verify: Users created with F-rank, BootcampLink created, eligibleForRealQuests = false

### Step 2: Verify quest visibility gating
- Log in as test student ? quest board should only show BOOTCAMP track quests
- Should only see Tutorial quests (eligibleForRealQuests = false)
- Attempting to view an INTERN quest via direct URL should return 403

### Step 3: Complete Tutorial Quest 1
- Create "Tutorial: First Blood" quest (BOOTCAMP track, F difficulty)
- Student claims quest ? assignment created with status "assigned"
- Student submits ? status goes to "pending_admin_review" (not "submitted")
- Admin reviews in QA queue ? approves ? status goes to "review" ? company approves ? "completed"
- Verify: tutorialQuest1Complete = true, XP granted, streak started

### Step 4: Complete Tutorial Quest 2
- Create "Tutorial: Party Up" quest (BOOTCAMP track, E difficulty)
- Same flow as Step 3
- Verify: tutorialQuest2Complete = true, eligibleForRealQuests = true

### Step 5: Access real quests
- Student should now see non-tutorial BOOTCAMP quests
- Claim a real quest, submit, go through QA mediation

### Step 6: Party formation (E-rank quest)
- Create E-rank BOOTCAMP quest with maxParticipants = 2
- Student 1 creates party ? auto-added as leader
- Student 1 adds Student 2 ? party full (BOOTCAMP max = 2)
- Both submit work

### Step 7: Rank-up verification
- After enough quests, verify F?E rank-up occurs
- XP bar shows correct progress
- Guild Card (when built) shows updated rank

## Expected Results
- [ ] All 5 students onboarded successfully
- [ ] Tutorial gating works (can't see real quests until both tutorials done)
- [ ] QA mediation flow works (pending_admin_review ? review)
- [ ] Party system works for BOOTCAMP pairs
- [ ] XP/rank progression works correctly
- [ ] No error states, no stuck assignments, no missing data

## Files Under Test
All Phase 0-2 code: onboard webhook, quest filtering, assignments, submissions, QA queue, XP utils, quest lifecycle, party system

## Owner
@LarytheLord — this is architecture validation, not contributor work

title: feat: Quest Board Filters — Track, Difficulty, Category, and Search

labels.name: contributor-friendly
labels.description: Well-documented issue suitable for new contributors

body:
## What

Enhance the quest board at \`/dashboard/quests\` with proper filtering, search, and sort controls.

## Current State

The quest board page (\`app/dashboard/quests/page.tsx\`) fetches all available quests and displays them in a grid. There's basic category filtering but no search, no difficulty filter, no track filter, and no sorting options.

## Requirements

### Filter Bar (above quest grid)

Add a horizontal filter bar with:

1. **Search** — text input that filters by quest title and description (client-side for now)
2. **Difficulty** — dropdown/chip selector: All / F / E / D / C / B / A / S (maps to \`quest.difficulty\`)
3. **Track** — dropdown/chip selector: All / Open / Intern / Bootcamp (maps to \`quest.track\`)
   - Note: Bootcamp students should only see BOOTCAMP quests (API already enforces this)
   - Admins see all tracks
4. **Category** — dropdown: All / plus existing \`QuestCategory\` enum values
5. **Sort** — dropdown: Newest / Highest XP / Highest Pay / Deadline Soon
6. **Active filter chips** — show selected filters as dismissible chips below the bar

### Quest API Enhancement

The existing \`GET /api/quests\` or quest listing may need:
- Query params: \`?track=BOOTCAMP&difficulty=E&category=FRONTEND&sort=xp_desc&search=react\`
- Server-side filtering is preferred for pagination, but client-side is acceptable for MVP

### Mobile
- Filter bar should be collapsible on mobile (tap "Filters" to expand)
- Or use a slide-out drawer pattern

## Design
- Use shadcn \`Select\`, \`Input\`, \`Badge\` components
- Filter chips: use \`Badge\` with an X button, orange-500 accent
- Keep the existing quest card grid layout
- Match the dark/light contrast rhythm of the dashboard

## Files to Modify
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| \`app/dashboard/quests/page.tsx\` | Modify — add filter bar + filter logic |
| \`app/api/quests/route.ts\` | Maybe modify — add server-side filter params |

## Existing Code Reference
- \`app/dashboard/quests/page.tsx\` — current quest board
- \`lib/quest-constants.ts\` — quest category list and helper functions
- \`prisma/schema.prisma\` — \`QuestCategory\`, \`QuestTrack\`, \`UserRank\` enums

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Search filters quests by title/description
- [ ] Difficulty filter works (F through S)
- [ ] Track filter works (OPEN/INTERN/BOOTCAMP)
- [ ] Category filter works
- [ ] Sort by XP, pay, deadline, newest
- [ ] Active filters shown as dismissible chips
- [ ] Mobile-responsive filter bar
- [ ] Type-check passes

title: feat: Party Panel UI — Squad Display on Quest Detail Page

labels.name: contributor-friendly
labels.description: Well-documented issue suitable for new contributors

body:
## What

Add a Party/Squad panel to the quest detail page that shows the current party members, allows the party leader to invite members, and displays party composition.

The **backend is already built** (Party + PartyMember models, 4 API routes). This issue is frontend only.

## Why

The Party System is central to delivery for both INTERN (squads of 3-5) and BOOTCAMP (pairs) tracks. Without a UI, the system is invisible to users.

## Existing API Routes (Already Built)

| Method | Route | What It Does |
|--------|-------|-------------|
| POST | \`/api/parties\` | Create party for a quest (auto-adds leader) |
| GET | \`/api/parties/[id]\` | Get party details with members |
| POST | \`/api/parties/[id]/members\` | Add member to party (leader/admin only) |
| DELETE | \`/api/parties/[id]/members/[userId]\` | Remove member (leader/admin, can't remove leader) |

The quest GET API (\`/api/quests/[id]\`) already includes party data:
\`\`\`json
{
  "quest": {
    "party": {
      "id": "...",
      "track": "INTERN",
      "maxSize": 5,
      "leader": { "id": "...", "name": "Alice", "rank": "C" },
      "members": [
        { "user": { "id": "...", "name": "Alice", "rank": "C" }, "isLeader": true },
        { "user": { "id": "...", "name": "Bob", "rank": "D" }, "isLeader": false }
      ]
    }
  }
}
\`\`\`

## Requirements

### On Quest Detail Page (\`app/dashboard/quests/[id]/page.tsx\`)

Add a new section **below the assignment panel** when quest has \`maxParticipants > 1\`:

**If no party exists yet (and user is assigned):**
- "Form a Party" button ? calls \`POST /api/parties\` with \`{ questId }\`
- Show: "This quest supports up to {maxParticipants} adventurers"

**If party exists:**
- Party card showing:
  - Party size: "3/5 members" with a progress indicator
  - Member list with: avatar, name, \`<RankBadge>\`, leader crown icon
  - Track badge (INTERN/BOOTCAMP)
- If current user is party leader:
  - "Invite Member" button ? opens a user search/input modal
  - "Remove" button next to non-leader members

### New Component: \`components/quest/PartyPanel.tsx\`

Props:
\`\`\`typescript
interface PartyPanelProps {
  questId: string;
  party: Party | null;
  maxParticipants: number;
  isAssigned: boolean;
  currentUserId: string;
  onPartyCreated: (party: Party) => void;
  onMemberAdded: () => void;
}
\`\`\`

## Design
- Use \`GuildCard\` + \`GuildPanel\` primitives (matches existing quest detail design)
- Leader gets a \`<Crown>\` icon (already imported in quest detail page)
- Member slots: show filled slots as user cards, empty slots as dashed outlines
- BOOTCAMP parties show "Pair" label, INTERN shows "Squad"

## Files to Create/Modify
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| \`components/quest/PartyPanel.tsx\` | Create — party display + management |
| \`app/dashboard/quests/[id]/page.tsx\` | Modify — integrate PartyPanel |

## Context
- APIs are in \`app/api/parties/\` — read the route files for exact request/response shapes
- Discussion #115 explains the party system design
- The Quest interface in the page already has the party relation data from the GET response

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Party panel appears on multi-participant quests
- [ ] "Form a Party" creates a party via API
- [ ] Member list shows with rank badges and leader crown
- [ ] Leader can add/remove members
- [ ] Shows party capacity (e.g., "2/5 members")
- [ ] Type-check passes

title: feat: Quest Streak System — Daily/Weekly XP Multiplier for Retention

labels.name: contributor-friendly
labels.description: Well-documented issue suitable for new contributors

body:
## What

Add a streak mechanic that rewards adventurers for consistent activity — completing quests on consecutive days/weeks earns bonus XP.

## Why

Retention is our biggest unknown. Streak mechanics are proven in gaming (Duolingo, Snapchat, every RPG ever) to drive daily return visits. For AG, this means adventurers come back to maintain their streak, which means more quest completions, which means more value for companies.

## Requirements

### Schema Changes (`prisma/schema.prisma`)

Add to `AdventurerProfile`:
```prisma
currentStreak     Int       @default(0) @map("current_streak")
longestStreak     Int       @default(0) @map("longest_streak")
lastActiveDate    DateTime? @map("last_active_date") @db.Date
streakMultiplier  Float     @default(1.0) @map("streak_multiplier")
```

### Streak Logic (`lib/streak-utils.ts`)

Create a utility that:
1. Is called after every quest completion (`QuestAssignment` status ? `completed`)
2. Checks `lastActiveDate` against today:
   - Same day: no change (already counted)
   - Yesterday: `currentStreak += 1`, update `lastActiveDate`
   - 2+ days ago: `currentStreak = 1` (streak broken), update `lastActiveDate`
3. Updates `longestStreak` if `currentStreak > longestStreak`
4. Calculates `streakMultiplier`:
   - Days 1-2: 1.0x (no bonus)
   - Days 3-6: 1.1x (10% bonus)
   - Days 7-13: 1.25x (25% bonus)
   - Days 14-29: 1.5x (50% bonus)
   - Days 30+: 2.0x (double XP)

### Integration

- Modify `lib/xp-utils.ts` ? `updateUserXpAndSkills()`: multiply XP reward by `streakMultiplier` before applying
- The streak should be visible on:
  - Adventurer dashboard (`app/dashboard/page.tsx`) — "?? 7-day streak! 1.25x XP" badge
  - Guild Card (issue #134) — show current streak if > 2

### API: `GET /api/adventurer/streak`

Auth: adventurer only. Returns:
```json
{
  "currentStreak": 7,
  "longestStreak": 14,
  "multiplier": 1.25,
  "lastActiveDate": "2026-03-20",
  "nextMilestone": { "days": 14, "multiplier": 1.5 }
}
```

## Files to Create/Modify
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `prisma/schema.prisma` | Modify — add streak fields to AdventurerProfile |
| `lib/streak-utils.ts` | Create — streak calculation logic |
| `lib/xp-utils.ts` | Modify — apply streak multiplier |
| `app/api/adventurer/streak/route.ts` | Create — streak API |
| `app/dashboard/page.tsx` | Modify — show streak badge |

## Context
- Setup: `docs/contributor-onboarding.md`
- XP system: `lib/xp-utils.ts` handles all XP grants
- Schema: `prisma/schema.prisma` is the source of truth
- Use `npx prisma db push` (NOT `prisma migrate dev`) — we use Neon serverless

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Streak increments on consecutive-day completions
- [ ] Streak resets after 2+ day gap
- [ ] XP multiplier applies correctly (check via quest completion)
- [ ] Dashboard shows streak count and multiplier
- [ ] API returns current streak data
- [ ] Type-check passes

title: feat: Admin Analytics Dashboard — DAU, Quest Metrics, Rank Distribution

labels.name: contributor-friendly
labels.description: Well-documented issue suitable for new contributors

body:
## What

Build an analytics dashboard at `/admin/analytics` that tracks the KPIs needed to prove product-market fit.

## Why

We currently have zero visibility into platform health — no retention data, no completion rates, no user activity trends. VCs and grant programs will ask for these numbers. More importantly, WE need them to know if the product is working.

## Requirements

### API: `GET /api/admin/analytics`

Auth: admin only (`requireAuth(request, 'admin')`)

Return these metrics:

**User Metrics:**
```json
{
  "totalUsers": 523,
  "activeUsersLast7d": 45,
  "activeUsersLast30d": 120,
  "newUsersLast7d": 12,
  "usersByRole": { "adventurer": 480, "company": 38, "admin": 5 },
  "rankDistribution": { "F": 300, "E": 120, "D": 40, "C": 15, "B": 4, "A": 1, "S": 0 }
}
```

**Quest Metrics:**
```json
{
  "totalQuests": 89,
  "availableQuests": 23,
  "completedQuests": 45,
  "questCompletionRate": 0.72,
  "avgTimeToComplete": 4.2,
  "questsByTrack": { "OPEN": 60, "INTERN": 20, "BOOTCAMP": 9 },
  "questsByDifficulty": { "F": 30, "E": 25, "D": 20, "C": 10, "B": 3, "A": 1 }
}
```

**Activity Metrics (last 30 days, daily buckets):**
```json
{
  "dailyActiveUsers": [{ "date": "2026-03-20", "count": 15 }, ...],
  "dailyQuestCompletions": [{ "date": "2026-03-20", "count": 3 }, ...],
  "dailySignups": [{ "date": "2026-03-20", "count": 2 }, ...]
}
```

### Implementation Notes

- "Active" = user has made any API request in the time window. Since we don't have an activity log yet, approximate using: `QuestAssignment.assignedAt`, `QuestSubmission.submittedAt`, `QuestCompletion.completedAt`, or `User.updatedAt` within the window.
- `avgTimeToComplete` = average days between `QuestAssignment.assignedAt` and `QuestAssignment.completedAt` for completed assignments
- Daily buckets: use Prisma `groupBy` with date truncation, or raw SQL `DATE_TRUNC('day', ...)` for Neon/PostgreSQL
- All queries should be efficient — use `count()`, `groupBy()`, and indexed fields

### Page: `app/admin/analytics/page.tsx`

- Match existing admin panel design (dark theme, see `/admin/page.tsx`)
- Cards for top-level KPIs (use `GuildKpi` primitives)
- Charts for daily trends — use `recharts` (already in dependencies)
- Rank distribution as a horizontal bar chart
- Quest pipeline funnel: available ? in_progress ? submitted ? completed
- Add navigation link from admin dashboard (`/admin/page.tsx` quick actions)

## Files to Create/Modify
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `app/api/admin/analytics/route.ts` | Create — analytics API |
| `app/admin/analytics/page.tsx` | Create — dashboard UI |
| `app/admin/page.tsx` | Modify — add quick action link |

## Existing Patterns to Follow
- Auth: `const user = await requireAuth(request, 'admin'); if (!user) return NextResponse.json({error:'Unauthorized'}, {status:401});`
- DB: `import { prisma } from '@/lib/db';`
- See `app/api/admin/qa-queue/route.ts` for a clean admin API example
- See `app/admin/qa-queue/page.tsx` for admin page patterns

## Context
- Discussion #117 covers the Phase 2 sprint plan
- The KPIs listed match the financial model in `FINANCIAL_MODEL.md` section 6
- `docs/contributor-onboarding.md` has the full setup guide

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] API returns all metrics listed above
- [ ] Dashboard renders charts and KPI cards
- [ ] Admin-only access enforced
- [ ] Type-check passes
- [ ] Works on mobile (responsive grid)

title: feat: Squad-Aware Quest Board UI — party slots, join flow, and squad filtering
body:
## Context

After the Squad/Party schema is built (issue #104), the quest board and detail pages need UI updates to expose party formation to adventurers. Currently the quest board shows quests as individual opportunities — Phase 3 surfaces the squad context.

**Prerequisite**: Issue #104 (Squad/Party System) must be merged before starting this.

Issue resolution rules: [`docs/ISSUE_RESOLUTION_GUIDE.md`](./docs/ISSUE_RESOLUTION_GUIDE.md)

---

## Quest Board Cards

File: `app/dashboard/quests/page.tsx` and related card components

**Changes to quest cards**:
- Show "Solo" badge for OPEN track quests
- Show "Party: X/Y" badge for INTERN/BOOTCAMP track quests with an active party
  - e.g. "Party: 2/5 members" in amber if not full, green if full
- Show "Open Party" badge if quest has no party yet (adventurer can form one)

---

## Quest Board Filter

Add a "Format" filter toggle alongside the existing filters:
- All
- Solo (OPEN track)
- Squad (INTERN/BOOTCAMP track)

This filter is hidden for bootcamp users (they only ever see BOOTCAMP quests).

---

## Quest Detail Party Panel (Enhancement over Issue #104)

Issue #104 built the Party Panel. This issue enhances it with:

**Party member cards** — each member shows:
- Avatar (initials if no profile photo)
- Name + RankBadge
- "Party Leader" tag if `isLeader: true`
- Join date (relative: "joined 2 days ago")

**"Invite to party" modal** (leader only):
- Search by adventurer name/email
- Shows matched users with rank badge
- "Invite" button sends a notification (Discord webhook) — no in-platform notification yet

---

## Files to Touch

| Action | File |
|--------|------|
| Modify | `app/dashboard/quests/page.tsx` — add party badges to cards |
| Modify | Quest card component — add party slot display |
| Modify | `app/dashboard/quests/[id]/page.tsx` — enhance Party Panel |
| Modify | Quest board filter component — add Format filter |

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Quest cards show "Solo", "Party: X/Y", or "Open Party" badge correctly
- [ ] Format filter (Solo/Squad) works and filters correctly
- [ ] Format filter hidden for bootcamp users
- [ ] Party member cards show avatar, name, rank, leader tag
- [ ] "Invite" modal (leader only) searches adventurers and sends Discord notification
- [ ] `npm run lint` ? 0 errors
- [ ] `npm run type-check` ? 0 errors
- [ ] `npm run build` ? passes

## What NOT to Do

- Do not build in-platform notifications — Discord is sufficient for Phase 3
- Do not add "request to join" functionality — invite-only
- Do not change the API — this is a UI-only issue (the API was built in #104)

title: feat: Hackathon ? Quest Continuation Pipeline — first-refusal window for hackathon teams

labels.name: C-rank
labels.description: Feature with schema changes or new API routes

body:
## Context

From the Open Paws Mentorship Programme (org plan):
> "Partner org becomes the client, hackathon team gets first right of refusal on the continuation quest in Adventurers Guild."

When a hackathon team builds something for a partner org, that org should have a direct path to commissioning a continuation quest — and the team that built the prototype should get first access before it hits the public board.

This issue implements:
1. Admin creates a quest pre-linked to a hackathon team (via user IDs)
2. Quest enters a 7-day "exclusive window" — visible only to linked team members
3. After 7 days (or team declines), quest becomes available to the public board

Issue resolution rules: [`docs/ISSUE_RESOLUTION_GUIDE.md`](./docs/ISSUE_RESOLUTION_GUIDE.md)

---

## Schema Changes

Add to `Quest`:
```prisma
exclusiveUntil         DateTime? @map("exclusive_until") @db.Timestamptz
exclusiveTeamUserIds   String[]  @map("exclusive_team_user_ids") @db.Uuid
```

`exclusiveUntil`: if set and in the future, quest is only visible to users in `exclusiveTeamUserIds`
`exclusiveTeamUserIds`: array of user IDs that can see/apply during exclusive window

```bash
npx prisma migrate dev --name add-quest-exclusive-window
```

---

## Admin Quest Creation Form

Add to the admin quest creation form (`/admin/create-quest` or wherever admin creates quests):

- "Exclusive window" toggle (optional)
- If enabled: date picker for `exclusiveUntil` (default: +7 days from today)
- "Team members" field: multi-user search/input for `exclusiveTeamUserIds`

---

## Quest Board Filtering Logic

In `GET /api/quests`, add exclusivity check:

```typescript
// A quest is visible if:
// 1. It has no exclusiveUntil (public), OR
// 2. exclusiveUntil is in the past (window expired), OR
// 3. The requesting user's ID is in exclusiveTeamUserIds
const now = new Date();
where.OR = [
  { exclusiveUntil: null },
  { exclusiveUntil: { lt: now } },
  { exclusiveTeamUserIds: { has: userId } }
];
```

---

## Decline Action

Add a "Decline exclusive offer" button on quest detail for team members during the exclusive window.

When clicked: removes the current user from `exclusiveTeamUserIds`. If all team members decline (array empty), set `exclusiveUntil = now` to immediately open the quest.

API: `PATCH /api/quests/[id]/decline-exclusive`

---

## Files to Touch

| Action | File |
|--------|------|
| Modify | `prisma/schema.prisma` |
| Modify | `app/api/quests/route.ts` — add exclusivity filter |
| Modify | Admin quest creation form — add exclusive window fields |
| Modify | `app/dashboard/quests/[id]/page.tsx` — show exclusive banner + decline button |
| Create | `app/api/quests/[id]/decline-exclusive/route.ts` |

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Schema fields added with migration applied
- [ ] Quest with active exclusive window is not visible to users not in `exclusiveTeamUserIds`
- [ ] Quest with expired `exclusiveUntil` appears on the public board normally
- [ ] Admin quest creation form accepts exclusive window configuration
- [ ] Quest detail page shows "Exclusive offer" banner during window for team members
- [ ] "Decline" removes user from team list; empty list opens the quest immediately
- [ ] `npm run lint` ? 0 errors
- [ ] `npm run type-check` ? 0 errors
- [ ] `npm run build` ? passes

## What NOT to Do

- Do not send email notifications — Discord webhook is sufficient
- Do not auto-assign the quest to the hackathon team — they still need to apply
- Do not add this feature to the company quest creation form — admin only

title: feat: Admin Revenue Dashboard — GMV, MRR, fill rate, and take rate charts
body:
## Context

Open Paws operates on a 15% commission model. As the platform scales to 20 interns and 50 bootcamp students, the team needs a real-time view of platform financial health.

Platform financial overview docs: `FINANCIAL_MODEL.md`, `MONETIZATION_ROADMAP.md`
Architecture context: `docs/IMPLEMENTATION_TASKS.md` — Task 3.C

Issue resolution rules: [`docs/ISSUE_RESOLUTION_GUIDE.md`](./docs/ISSUE_RESOLUTION_GUIDE.md)

---

## New Route: `GET /api/admin/revenue`

Auth: `admin` only

Returns financial metrics for a given time range.

```typescript
// Query params: ?from=2026-01-01&to=2026-03-31
// Response:
{
  gmv: number,                  // total quest rewards processed (sum of Transaction.amount)
  platformRevenue: number,      // sum of Transaction.platformFee
  takeRate: number,             // platformRevenue / gmv
  fillRate: number,             // completed quests / total posted quests
  questsPosted: number,
  questsCompleted: number,
  activeAdventurers: number,    // users with at least 1 assignment in range
  avgQuestValue: number,        // gmv / questsCompleted
  byTrack: {
    INTERN: { gmv, count },
    BOOTCAMP: { gmv, count },
    OPEN: { gmv, count }
  },
  timeline: Array<{             // 30 data points for chart
    date: string,
    gmv: number,
    questsCompleted: number
  }>
}
```

---

## New Page: `/admin/revenue`

File: `app/admin/revenue/page.tsx`

Use `recharts` (already a dependency — check `package.json`) for charts.

### Layout

**Top row — KPI cards**:
| GMV | Platform Revenue | Take Rate | Fill Rate |
|-----|-----------------|-----------|-----------|
| ?4,20,000 | ?63,000 | 15% | 72% |

**Middle row — charts**:
- Left: 30-day GMV line chart
- Right: Quests completed bar chart (grouped by track)

**Bottom row — tables**:
- Top quests by value (title, track, reward, completion date)
- Top adventurers by earnings (name, rank, total earned)

---

## Navigation

Add "Revenue" link to admin sidebar nav.

---

## Files to Touch

| Action | File |
|--------|------|
| Create | `app/api/admin/revenue/route.ts` |
| Create | `app/admin/revenue/page.tsx` |
| Modify | Admin sidebar nav — add Revenue link |

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `GET /api/admin/revenue` returns GMV, platform revenue, take rate, fill rate
- [ ] `byTrack` breakdown included in response
- [ ] `timeline` array has 30 data points for chart rendering
- [ ] `/admin/revenue` page shows KPI cards with correct values
- [ ] GMV line chart renders with recharts
- [ ] Quests completed bar chart renders grouped by track
- [ ] Admin sidebar has Revenue link
- [ ] `npm run lint` ? 0 errors
- [ ] `npm run type-check` ? 0 errors
- [ ] `npm run build` ? passes

## What NOT to Do

- Do not add MRR until subscription plans (Task 3.E) are implemented — hardcode 0 for now
- Do not use a third-party charting library other than recharts (already installed)
- Do not make this page accessible to non-admin roles

title: feat: Quest Event Audit Trail — EventLog model and admin quest timeline

labels.name: C-rank
labels.description: Feature with schema changes or new API routes

body:
## Context

Read: [Platform Architecture Overview](https://github.com/LarytheLord/Adventurers-Guild/discussions/112) — quest and assignment lifecycle.

**Phase 3 issue** — not on the critical path. Pick this up after Phase 2 issues (#104–#107) are merged.

---

## What to Build

Every quest state transition (created, started, submitted, approved, rejected, etc.) gets logged to an `EventLog` table. Admin can see a full timeline on any quest's detail page.

This creates a permanent audit trail useful for:
- Dispute resolution (who did what and when)
- Pattern detection (quests that frequently get rejected)
- Student progress tracking

---

## Schema

\`\`\`prisma
model EventLog {
  id         String   @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  questId    String?  @map("quest_id") @db.Uuid
  userId     String   @map("user_id") @db.Uuid
  action     String   // "quest_created", "assignment_approved", "submission_rejected", etc.
  fromState  String?  @map("from_state")
  toState    String?  @map("to_state")
  metadata   Json?
  createdAt  DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at") @db.Timestamptz

  quest Quest? @relation(fields: [questId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
  user  User   @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])

  @@index([questId])
  @@index([userId])
  @@index([createdAt])
  @@map("event_logs")
}
\`\`\`

Add to `Quest` model:
\`\`\`prisma
eventLogs EventLog[]
\`\`\`

Add to `User` model:
\`\`\`prisma
eventLogs EventLog[]
\`\`\`

Run: `npx prisma db push`

---

## What Actions to Log

Add `EventLog.create()` calls inside the existing transaction blocks of these routes:

| Route | Trigger | Action string |
|-------|---------|---------------|
| `POST /api/quests` | Quest created | `quest_created` |
| `POST /api/quests/assignments` | Adventurer applies | `assignment_created` |
| `PUT /api/quests/[id]/assignments` | Company accepts | `assignment_accepted` |
| `POST /api/quests/submissions` | Submission posted | `submission_posted` |
| `PATCH /api/admin/qa-queue/[id]` | Admin approves | `qa_approved` |
| `PATCH /api/admin/qa-queue/[id]` | Admin rejects | `qa_rejected` |
| `PUT /api/quests/submissions` | Client approves | `submission_approved` |
| `PUT /api/quests/submissions` | Client rejects (rework) | `submission_rejected` |

Store `fromState` and `toState` whenever a status changes. `metadata` can hold extra context (e.g. reviewer name, notes truncated to 100 chars).

---

## Admin Quest Timeline UI

On `/admin/quests/[id]` (existing page), add a "Timeline" section below the quest details:

\`\`\`
Timeline

? Quest created                           Mar 17, 2026 10:00
? Amir K applied                          Mar 17, 2026 10:15
? Application accepted                    Mar 17, 2026 11:00
? Submission posted                       Mar 18, 2026 14:30
? QA approved — forwarded to client       Mar 19, 2026 09:00
? Client approved — XP granted            Mar 19, 2026 16:45
\`\`\`

Fetch from: `GET /api/admin/quests/[id]/events` ? returns EventLogs for that questId ordered by `createdAt asc`.

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `EventLog` model in schema with migration
- [ ] Events logged for all actions listed above
- [ ] `GET /api/admin/quests/[id]/events` returns chronological event list
- [ ] Admin quest detail page shows timeline section
- [ ] `npm run build` passes
- [ ] `npm run type-check` passes

## Rules

- Target branch: `Phase-2` (branch off from it, PR back to it)
- Run `npm run build` and `npm run type-check` before opening a PR — zero errors required
- Do NOT add EventLog calls to routes that don't already exist — only log in routes that are already implemented
- Keep logging lightweight — no blocking calls, always log inside existing transactions when possible

title: feat: Stripe Connect — Adventurer payout onboarding and PaymentIntent flow
body:
## Context

Read these discussions first:
- [Payment Architecture](https://github.com/LarytheLord/Adventurers-Guild/discussions/118) — what already exists vs what to build, Stripe Connect Express flow, BOOTCAMP exclusion rule
- [Open Paws Ecosystem](https://github.com/LarytheLord/Adventurers-Guild/discussions/113) — why interns need real payouts by May

**Depends on**: nothing. Can be picked up now in parallel with other Phase 2 work.

---

## What to Build

Wire Stripe Connect Express so adventurers can receive real payouts on quest approval.

---

## Schema Changes

Add to `AdventurerProfile` in `prisma/schema.prisma`:
\`\`\`prisma
stripeAccountId      String?  @map("stripe_account_id")
stripeOnboardingDone Boolean  @default(false) @map("stripe_onboarding_done")
\`\`\`

Add to `CompanyProfile`:
\`\`\`prisma
stripeCustomerId String? @map("stripe_customer_id")
\`\`\`

Add to `Transaction`:
\`\`\`prisma
stripePaymentIntentId String? @map("stripe_payment_intent_id")
platformFee           Int     @default(0) @map("platform_fee")
platformFeeRate       Float   @default(0.15) @map("platform_fee_rate")
\`\`\`

Run: `npx prisma db push`

---

## New API Routes

### `POST /api/payments/stripe/connect`
Auth: adventurer

1. Check `adventurerProfile.stripeAccountId` — if already set, return it
2. Call `stripe.accounts.create({ type: 'express', email: user.email })`
3. Call `stripe.accountLinks.create({ account: accountId, type: 'account_onboarding', ... })`
4. Return `{ onboardingUrl }`

### `GET /api/payments/stripe/connect/callback`
Auth: adventurer (from Stripe redirect)

Query param: `?account=acct_xxx` (Stripe sends this on return)

1. Get `stripeAccountId` from query or fetch from `stripe.accounts.retrieve` using session user
2. Verify account is enabled (`details_submitted === true`)
3. Update `adventurerProfile.stripeAccountId` + `stripeOnboardingDone = true`
4. Redirect to `/dashboard/settings?stripe=success`

### `POST /api/payments/stripe/intent`
Auth: company or admin

Body: `{ questId: string, amount: number, currency: string }`

1. Lookup company's `stripeCustomerId` (create if missing)
2. Create `PaymentIntent` for `amount` with `application_fee_amount = amount * 0.15`
3. Return `{ clientSecret }`

---

## Dashboard Settings Page

Add payout setup to `/dashboard/settings` (or create it if it doesn't exist):

\`\`\`
Payout Setup
[ ] Bank account connected via Stripe

[Set up payouts ?]   ? calls POST /api/payments/stripe/connect, redirects to onboardingUrl
\`\`\`

After successful callback, show:
\`\`\`
? Payouts active — bank account connected
\`\`\`

---

## Critical Rule

**BOOTCAMP track quests must NEVER trigger a PaymentIntent.** Gate like this:

\`\`\`typescript
if (quest.track === 'BOOTCAMP') {
  // XP only — skip payment
  return;
}
\`\`\`

---

## Fallback

When `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` is not set, the existing simulated payment flow (`txn_${Date.now()}`) must continue to work. Do not break it.

---

## Files to Touch

- `prisma/schema.prisma` — add fields above
- `lib/stripe.ts` — already exists, singleton is ready
- `app/api/payments/stripe/connect/route.ts` — create
- `app/api/payments/stripe/connect/callback/route.ts` — create
- `app/api/payments/stripe/intent/route.ts` — create
- `app/dashboard/settings/page.tsx` — add payout section

---

## Env Vars Required (use test keys locally)

\`\`\`
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_...
STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk_test_...
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_...
NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk_test_...
\`\`\`

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `stripeAccountId` + `stripeOnboardingDone` added to `AdventurerProfile` schema
- [ ] `POST /api/payments/stripe/connect` returns valid Stripe onboarding URL
- [ ] Callback route saves `stripeAccountId`, marks `stripeOnboardingDone = true`
- [ ] Dashboard settings page shows payout setup status + button
- [ ] BOOTCAMP track quest approval does NOT trigger PaymentIntent
- [ ] Existing simulated payment flow still works when Stripe not configured
- [ ] `npm run build` passes
- [ ] `npm run type-check` passes

## Rules

- Target branch: `Phase-2` (branch off from it, PR back to it)
- Run `npm run build` and `npm run type-check` before opening a PR — zero errors required
- Do not break existing payment flow
- Do not add Stripe to BOOTCAMP quests
